content.Text: [Mon 2026-04-20 01:37 PDT] Create a new interactive HTML tool at docs/storyteller.html for GraphVisual — a "Graph Storyteller" that autonomously analyzes a graph and generates a rich natural-language narrative about its structure. ## What it does Users input a graph (adjacency list text, or pick from 8 presets). The tool then runs multiple analyses and produces a multi-paragraph "story" about the graph — written in engaging prose, not just stats. It covers: 1. **Opening** — graph overview (nodes, edges, density, type) 2. **Characters** — identifies hub nodes (highest degree), bridges, isolates. Names them as "protagonists", "connectors", "loners" 3. **Communities** — runs label propagation to find groups, describes them 4. **Structure** — connected components, cycles, bipartiteness, diameter, clustering coefficient 5. **Drama** — identifies vulnerabilities (bridges, articulation points as "single points of failure"), bottlenecks 6. **Prediction** — based on structure, suggests what might happen if the network grows (preferential attachment trends) 7. **Verdict** — one-line characterization of the graph's "personality" (e.g. "A tightly-knit small world" or "A fragile hub-and-spoke network") ## UI Layout Use the same sidebar nav pattern as other docs pages (link to styles.css, include the full sidebar). Use the same CSS variable system (--surface, --bg, --accent, --border, --text, --text-muted, etc.). Layout: left panel (300px) with: - Textarea for adjacency list input (format: "A-B" per line) - 8 preset buttons: Karate Club, Star, Ring, Complete K6, Binary Tree, Petersen, Grid 4x4, Random(15,25) - "Tell the Story" primary button - Narration speed slider (controls typing animation speed) - Export buttons: Copy Text, Download Markdown Right panel: the story output area styled like a book page with: - Typing animation effect (characters appear one by one) - Section headers with emojis - Key stats highlighted in accent color - Node names as clickable pills that highlight in a small Canvas graph at the top of the story area Include a small Canvas visualization (300px tall) at the top of the story panel showing the graph with force-directed layout. Nodes mentioned in the current story section should pulse/highlight. ## Presets data - Karate Club: Zachary's karate club (34 nodes, use the standard edge list) - Star: center node connected to 8 leaves - Ring: 12 nodes in a cycle - Complete K6: complete graph on 6 nodes - Binary Tree: depth 4 (15 nodes) - Petersen: the Petersen graph (10 nodes, 15 edges) - Grid 4x4: 4x4 mesh - Random(15,25): random 15 nodes, 25 edges ## Algorithms to implement (all client-side JS) - Degree distribution, density - BFS for connected components and diameter - Label propagation for communities - Bridge detection (Tarjan) - Articulation point detection - Clustering coefficient (local average) - Bipartiteness check (BFS 2-coloring) - Degree centrality ## Story generation Write the narrative generator as a function that takes analysis results and produces engaging prose. Use varied vocabulary. Include specific numbers and node names. Make it read like a network science blog post, not a table of stats. ## Sidebar nav Copy the full sidebar from docs/index.html but add a new entry for this tool: ```html 📖Graph Storyteller ``` Place it after the Graph Evolution entry. ## Technical notes - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no external dependencies - Link to styles.css for base theme - Use Canvas API for the graph visualization - File goes at docs/storyteller.html - Make sure all JavaScript is syntactically valid After creating the file, also update docs/index.html to add the storyteller link in the sidebar Tools section (after Evolution Sim): ```html 📖Graph Storyteller ``` Verify with: node -e "require('fs').readFileSync('docs/storyteller.html','utf8')" to confirm the file is readable.
